raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this non-duplicative complaint against Barclays Bank Delaware for their failure to validate, investigate, or resolve a fraudulent {$8500.00} charge on my account ending in XXXX, stemming from a transaction with XXXX XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX I paid {$8500.00}, but no goods or services were delivered, constituting fraud. On XX/XX/XXXX, I sent Barclays a demand letter requesting validation, a detailed investigation outline, and a refund, but Barclays responded that " no new information exists, '' refusing to comply. This deliberate indifference violates multiple laws, causing financial harm and credit damage. 

Fraudulent Charge : The {$8500.00} charge relates to a transaction with XXXX XXXX XXXX, who, along with XXXX XXXX, failed to deliver any goods or services ( XXXX XXXX XXXX ), rendering the debt fraudulent without validation. 
Barclays Failures : Barclays has not provided validation ( e.g., signed agreement, proof of delivery ), an investigation outline, or any evidence to support the debts legitimacy, despite my XX/XX/XXXX demand. Their claim of " no new information '' dismisses their legal obligations, showing deliberate indifference. 
Impact : This unresolved fraudulent debt harms my credit score, causes financial distress, and denies me rightful access to the {$8500.00} stolen by XXXX XXXX XXXX. 
Prior Actions, which did not address this specific validation and investigation failure. 
Legal Violations : FCRA 1681s-2 ( b ) : Barclays must investigate disputes within 30 days ( due XX/XX/XXXX ) ; their failure to provide an investigation outline violates this section, justifying removal from credit reports. 
FCRA 1681s-2 ( e ) : Barclays must disclose verification procedures within 15 days ( due XX/XX/XXXX ) ; their refusal to provide this information breaches transparency requirements. 
FDCPA 1692g : Barclays and must validate the debt within 30 days of my dispute ; their failure to do so, as affirmed by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ), requires cessation of collection. 
FDCPA 1692e : Claiming " no new information exists '' while pursuing an unvalidated debt misrepresents its legitimacy, violating this section.

FCBA 1666 : I dispute this {$8500.00} as a billing error ( no goods received ) ; Barclays failure to investigate and refund violates FCBA.

FTC Act 5 : Barclays deliberate indifference to fraud by XXXX XXXX XXXX is an unfair practice, causing substantial harm. 
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX ) : Pursuing a fraudulent debt without validation or investigation is a deceptive practice under Ohio law.
